pairs.distinction: Duty defines required conduct in advance; responsibility assigns answerability once conduct, omission, or outcome is attributable.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat required conduct and answerable attribution as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between duty and responsibility is not admitted.
conflictCauses: A duty claim assigns identityKind answerable_attribution.
A responsibility claim assigns identityKind required_conduct.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between duty and responsibility.

pairs.distinction: Duty is the required conduct line before assessment; violation is the assessed state in which that required line is unmet.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat required conduct and unmet-duty state as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between duty and violation is not admitted.
conflictCauses: A duty claim assigns identityKind unmet_duty_state.
A violation claim assigns identityKind required_conduct.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between duty and violation.

pairs.distinction: Responsibility is answerability for attributable conduct or outcome; violation is the unmet-duty state that may later ground answerability.
cannotCoexist: The same governance claim cannot treat answerable attribution and unmet-duty state as the same identity slot.
An equivalent_to relation between responsibility and violation is not admitted.
conflictCauses: A responsibility claim assigns identityKind unmet_duty_state.
A violation claim assigns identityKind answerable_attribution.
A claim asserts equivalent_to between responsibility and violation.
